food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal set (burger patty, potato fries, carbonated beverage)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a food product; this record is a meal set including burger, fries, and drink. Closest defensible anchor used; no exact meal-set code available in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers hamburgers as a category. The full meal set (burger + fries + drink) has no single FoodEx2 code; burger component anchor used as primary ontology reference.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for fast food burger meal set. SNOMED CT clinical food coding does not extend to branded fast food meal combinations at this specificity.

preparation_method: assembled burger with creamy mushroom sauce

serving.description: 1 meal set (burger + medium fries + medium drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set consisting of one Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger (approximately 220 g), one medium portion of Star Potatoes / fries (approximately 150 g), and one medium carbonated soft drink (approximately 250 ml / 250 g), totalling approximately 620 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fast Foods, Hamburger, large, single patty, with condiments and vegetables (FDC ID 21318) combined with USDA FDC Fast Food French Fried Potatoes (FDC ID 21138) and estimated beverage component; adapted for creamy mushroom sauce addition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger Meal as a combined set. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted composition: burger component (~35% of meal weight) anchored to USDA FDC 21318 (fast food large hamburger with condiments, ~270 kcal/100g); fries component (~24% of meal weight) anchored to USDA FDC 21138 (fast food french fried potatoes, ~323 kcal/100g); creamy mushroom sauce addition increases fat and sodium modestly; carbonated soft drink component (~40% of meal weight) anchored to USDA FDC 14400 (carbonated beverage, cola, ~37 kcal/100g). Weighted blended per-100g values calculated across 620 g total meal weight. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Fast food meal with refined bun and fried potatoes analogous estimate; hamburger bun GI ~72, french fries GI ~63, cola GI ~63, blended weighted estimate ~66.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger Meal as a combined set. GI estimated by weighted blending of approved GI database values: hamburger bun (GI 72, University of Sydney GI Database), french fries (GI 63, Atkinson et al. 2008), and cola beverage (GI 63, University of Sydney GI Database), weighted by carbohydrate contribution across meal components. Blended GI approximately 66 (medium category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (66 × 151) / 100 ≈ 99.5, where carbohydrate per serving = 24.5 g/100g × 620 g / 100 = 151.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg/100 g; ~1922 mg per meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 1922 mg sodium — exceeding 80% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — primarily from the burger patty seasoning, creamy mushroom sauce, and salted fries.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~99.5 per meal set) and high refined carbohydrate content from bun, fries, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal, as the combination of a refined wheat bun, deep-fried potatoes, and a sugar-sweetened cola produces a very high glycemic load of approximately 99.5 per serving, which is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.6 g/100 g; ~16 g per meal), trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in saturated fat, contains trans fat from deep-frying, and delivers a high sodium load, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with CVD or at elevated cardiovascular risk should limit consumption to occasional treats and consider lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger Meal is a fast food meal set sold at McDonald's Singapore, consisting of a beef burger topped with a creamy mushroom sauce and sautéed mushrooms on a toasted wheat bun, served alongside a portion of Star Potatoes (seasoned deep-fried potato fries) and a medium carbonated soft drink. The burger patty is grilled or pan-fried beef, and the creamy mushroom sauce provides a rich, savoury, umami-forward flavour profile with a smooth, velvety texture. The fries are crispy and lightly salted, while the cola or soft drink adds sweetness and effervescence to the meal. The meal is certified Halal and is a popular fast food option in Singapore. Nutritionally, the full meal set (~620 g) delivers approximately 1147 kcal, 42 g protein, 45 g fat (including ~16 g saturated fat), and ~152 g carbohydrates, with a high sodium content of approximately 1922 mg per serving. The glycemic load per meal is very high (~99.5), driven by the refined bun, fried potatoes, and sugar-sweetened beverage. This meal is not recommended for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and glycemic load. It is classified as ultra-processed and is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet.

Is the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger Halal?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe Burger Meal as a combined set was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition from USDA FDC approved entries for fast food hamburger (FDC 21318), fast food french fries (FDC 21138), and cola beverage (FDC 14400), adjusted for the creamy mushroom sauce component. GI is an analogous estimate from approved University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed values. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the full meal set (burger + fries + drink, ~620 g total). Per-serving values are approximately 6.2× the per-100g values. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FDC source entries used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported across the component USDA FDC entries at sufficient reliability for this adapted estimate. 5. The food_group in the input JSON was listed as 'Vegetables', which is incorrect for a burger meal set; corrected to 'Mixed' per Veritas classification rules. 6. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The meal is sold at McDonald's Singapore (Halal-certified) and is available at multiple outlets including Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay branches. 8. trans_fat_g of 0.2 g/100g is an estimate from deep-frying; actual value may vary by batch and oil type.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record from input. Identified food_group 'Vegetables' as incorrect; corrected to 'Mixed'. Added all mandatory multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Built nutrient profile from USDA FDC weighted composition. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for g5_ontology_honesty. Corrected `foodon_match_type` and `foodex2_match_type` from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to align with allowed values. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Verified -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Verified no zero core macros. Verified GI > 0. Confirmed all hard gates pass and score 8/8.
